reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff date (May 6, 2025), there have been no public accusations by the US government that China is actively blockading a Taiwanese seaport. While China has conducted intensive military drills around Taiwan (including "Strait Thunder-2025A" in April), which the US criticized and which involved simulated/rehearsal blockades, these do not constitute an actual blockade of a seaport. With less than two months remaining until the resolution date of July 1, 2025, the likelihood of a sudden escalation to a full maritime blockade—and the subsequent US accusation—is extremely low, as this would be an act of war and trigger a major global crisis. Therefore, the probability is assessed at the minimum threshold of a highly unlikely event.
